timeline.compressToSummary: AI Compress: merge selected events into a summary

characters.noRelationships: No relationships recorded yet. AI will record them as characters interact.

items.title: Item Tracking List
items.longPressHint: Tap the top multi-select button to select items

locations.title: Scene Memory
locations.noLocationsHint: Enable 'Settings → Scene Memory' and AI will record location features on first visit.

rpg.userOnlyHint: Toggle: all RPG modules track USER only (can also set per module)
rpg.strictPresentHint: Do not send RPG data when characters_present is empty

tail.name: Tail
tail.desc: Tail gear or ornaments; unsuitable for forms without a wearable tail

wings.name: Wings
wings.desc: Wing guards, feather ornaments or flight aids

tailOrnament.name: Tail Ornament
tailOrnament.desc: Usually one ornament per wearable tail

serpentTailOrnament.name: Serpent Tail Ornament
serpentTailOrnament.desc: Serpent tail guards, rings or gear fitted to a snake tail

hp.name: HP
hp.desc: Health; changes through injury, poison, healing and rest

mp.name: MP
mp.desc: Mana; changes through casting, mana use, meditation or recovery items

sp.name: SP
sp.desc: Stamina; changes through running, dodging, melee bursts, fatigue and rest

str.name: Strength
str.desc: Physical attack, carrying capacity and melee damage

settings.configProfileHint: Export all toggles, protagonist-only settings, custom prompts, RPG config as a file (excludes auto-summary & vector settings). Card authors can share configs for one-click setup.
settings.autoFillPrevTimelineOnSend: Auto-fill previous AI message timeline before send
settings.autoFillPrevTimelineOnSendHint: Before sending, if the previous AI reply is missing <horae>/<horaeevent>, Horae performs a full AI analysis on that message using lightweight state, character identity, and the previous user action as context, then writes all extracted data back to chat history. This calls AI and is off by default.
settings.useMainPresetForAiTasks: Use Tavern main preset for background AI tasks
settings.useMainPresetForAiTasksHint: Affects Horae background AI tasks such as AI Smart Summary, single-message analysis, and manual/auto summarization. Off by default: tasks run on a parallel channel and auto-inject your active main/NSFW/jailbreak prompts from your Tavern preset, without blocking the main chat. When enabled, tasks switch to generate('quiet') with the full preset (incl. world info / character card / history), but they run serially with the main chat. Enable only if the default jailbreak still fails or output is abnormal.
settings.showTopIconHint: When off, the top icon is completely hidden. Re-enable via Extensions panel → Horae Memory.
settings.panelWidthHint: Panel width as % of chat area (50-100). Reduce for widescreen themes. Right offset fine-tunes horizontal position.
settings.themeHint: Choose a built-in theme or import custom themes. Imported theme files (.json) appear in the dropdown.
settings.customCssHint: Enter custom CSS to override plugin styles. For advanced users. Changes apply on blur.
settings.contextDepthHint:
Controls how many 'Normal' level events are sent to AI:
0 = No normal events (Important/Critical still sent)
15 = Last 15 normal events (default)
Higher values = more tokens consumed
settings.injectionPositionHint:
Where Horae injects state info in AI prompts:
0 = End (closest to latest message)
1 = Second from end (recommended)
Higher = further up
settings.injectionDepthHint:
Choose the @D source and set the depth value.
System @D = use system-layer depth
Preset @D = use preset-layer depth
settings.timelineInjectionInlineHint: Keeps the story timeline in the same injection block as state data.
settings.timelineInjectionSeparateHint: Places the story timeline separately and earlier in the chat context.
settings.timelineInjectionModeHint: Original merged injection keeps the story timeline in the same block as state data. Separate early injection places the story timeline earlier in the prompt.
settings.sendToAiHint: Control what info is sent with prompts. Disabling saves tokens but AI won't 'remember' that info.
settings.sendTimelineHint: Event summaries & relative time. Without it, AI can't know what happened 'days ago'.
settings.sendCharactersHint: Present characters, costumes, affection, NPC info. Without it, AI may describe wrong outfits.
settings.sendItemsHint: Currently held items & locations. Without it, AI may create items from nothing.
settings.sendLocationMemoryHint: Records fixed physical features of locations for consistent scene descriptions across turns.
settings.sendRelationshipsHint: Tracks relationship changes between characters (friends, lovers, enemies, etc.).
settings.sendMoodHint: Tracks emotional/psychological state changes for consistent character emotions.
settings.rpgModeHint: When enabled, AI outputs character stats (HP/MP/SP) and skill changes in <horaerpg> tags.
settings.antiParaphraseHint: For play styles where USER writes their own actions. AI's <horae> tags will also account for the previous USER message.
settings.sideplayModeHint: Adds a 'Mark as side story' button. Marked messages are not tracked by Horae or included in auto-summaries.
settings.autoSummaryHint:
When unsummarized content reaches the trigger threshold, old events are auto-compressed into AI summaries to save tokens.
Compressed summaries can be expanded back to original events.
settings.customTablesHint:
Create Excel-style tables for AI to fill. Long-press headers to add/remove rows/columns.
Local tables save per-chat; Character tables bind to a character card (structure follows the card, data is per-chat); Global tables are shared.
Click the scope icon to switch. 🔒 Lock rows/columns to prevent AI edits.
settings.uiLanguageHint: Plugin UI display language. "Auto Detect" selects based on browser/tavern settings.
settings.aiOutputLanguageHint: Controls the language AI uses in horae tags (event summaries, NPC descriptions, etc.). Format markers stay unchanged, only descriptive text is affected.
settings.keepRecentHint: The last N AI messages are never summarized and are always sent in full. Interleaved user messages are included but not counted.
settings.autoSummarySourceHint: Full text + timeline events keeps more detail, but uses more tokens and is easier to truncate. Timeline events only saves tokens.
settings.bufferLimitHint: Auto-summary triggers when unsummarized content reaches the current threshold.
settings.resummaryThresholdHint: When second-level summaries reach this count, they will be merged into third-level summaries, and so on.
settings.autoSummaryExample:
💡 Example: if Keep is 5 and the summary threshold is 12, auto-summary runs when the AI message count reaches 17. The summary will cover the previous 12 AI messages.
Because regular Tavern chats also include user messages, in floor terms this roughly means that when you reach floor 34, the previous 24 floors are summarized.
In Token mode, the token threshold is used instead.
settings.batchMaxMessagesHint: Max messages per auto-summary batch. Large archives will be processed in multiple rounds.
settings.batchMaxTokensHint:
Token limit per summary batch; excess is truncated.
Refer to model context windows (recommend 30%-50%):
Claude ≈ 80K-200K · GPT-4o ≈ 80K-128K · Gemini ≈ 100K-1000K · DeepSeek ≈ 64K
settings.useIndependentApiHint: Run background analysis and summaries through an independent endpoint to reduce main API rate pressure.
settings.summaryApiUrlHint: OpenAI-compatible endpoint base URL, e.g. https://api.openai.com/v1
settings.summaryFetchModelsHint: Fill in URL and key, then click to fetch. A low-cost model will suffice.
settings.subApiHint: Auxiliary API requests are sent serially to avoid endpoint concurrency limits. The connection test only queries the model list.
settings.auxApiEnabledHint: Run background analysis and summaries through an independent endpoint to reduce main API rate pressure.
settings.auxApiUrlHint: OpenAI-compatible endpoint base URL, e.g. https://api.openai.com/v1
settings.auxApiSecretHint: Not exported with Horae config profiles.
settings.auxApiFetchModelsHint: Fill in URL and key, then click to fetch available models.
settings.auxApiUseForAnalysis: AI analysis, magic wand, and pre-send fill
settings.auxApiManualCompressHint: If the auxiliary API cannot bypass limits, disable this and use the native Tavern channel with the main preset.
settings.auxApiFallbackToMain: Fallback to main API if auxiliary API fails
settings.auxApiFallbackHint: Off by default to avoid consuming main API rate limit after auxiliary API failure.
settings.auxApiHint: Auxiliary API requests are sent serially to avoid endpoint concurrency limits. The connection test only queries the model list.

badge.sideplayMarked: This message is marked as a side story

tutorial.step1Content:
This plugin lets AI automatically track story states.
Horae attaches <horae> tags to AI replies, auto-recording time, scenes, characters, items and more.

Let me walk you through the core features. Follow the prompts.
tutorial.stepStatusContent:
The Status page is an overview of the current story state. It shows the current time, location, scene summary and present characters at a glance.

The weekday is calculated automatically from the real calendar date. It is not a separately editable field; if the date and weekday do not match, fix the date itself.

Ready out of the box: for new conversations, you do not need to create tables first. Horae maintains these core states automatically as AI replies.
tutorial.stepTimelineContent:
The Event Timeline is Horae's core. The <horae> records generated by AI replies are organized here as events, powering Auto Summary, Vector Memory and the story trajectory sent back to AI.

Disabling the timeline is not recommended, or the plugin's long-term memory will be significantly weakened.
tutorial.stepCharactersContent:
The Characters page organizes present characters, other characters and their state changes, so you can check who is present, who left and what each character's current state is.

This is built-in Horae tracking. You do not need to recreate it with Custom Tables.
tutorial.stepItemsContent:
The Items page records important story items, ownership, location and state changes.

It is separate from RPG equipment and Custom Tables. Basic item tracking works automatically and does not require extra table setup.
tutorial.step2Content:
If you have old chat logs, use 'AI Smart Summary' to batch-fill <horae> tags first.
AI will re-read history and generate structured timeline data.

New conversations need no action — the plugin works automatically.

When a high-floor chat gets too slow: use 'Create New Chat with Memory' to carry the accumulated state into a fresh chat, including final states for characters, items, relationships, RPG, Custom Tables, Scene Memory and more, plus an old-plot recap and recent messages. Note: older original message text is not fully carried over, so Vector Memory full-text recall/review for old messages will be affected.
tutorial.step3Content:
Auto Summary is off by default. When enabled, old messages that reach the trigger threshold are auto-summarized and hidden, saving tokens.

The Auto-summary Source defaults to Full text + timeline events for richer detail. You can switch to Timeline events only to save tokens. The trigger threshold can count AI messages or tokens, and each mode keeps its own value.

Note: This requires existing timeline data (<horae> tags). For old records, use 'AI Smart Summary' first before enabling this.
· If auto-summary keeps failing, go to the Event Timeline and manually multi-select + full-text summarize.
tutorial.step4Content:
A recall feature for Auto Summary users. After compression, old message details are lost. Vector memory auto-retrieves relevant snippets from hidden timelines when conversations touch past events.

Should you enable it?
· If you use Auto Summary with many messages → recommended
· If you don't use Auto Summary, few messages, plenty of tokens → not needed

Source options:
· Local Model: Runs in browser, no API costs. First use downloads a ~30-60MB model.
⚠️ OOM Warning: Local models may cause page freeze/blank screen from memory issues. Switch to API mode if this happens.
· API: Uses a remote Embedding model (not your LLM chat model). Supports OpenAI Embeddings and Gemini v1beta/batchEmbedContents; for official Gemini, enter the Google Generative Language API base URL.
Recommended: Free Embedding models from SiliconFlow (e.g. BAAI/bge-m3).

Fulltext Review: High-confidence results can send original message text for richer context. Adjust count and threshold freely; set to 0 to disable.
tutorial.step5Content:
Controls how many timeline events are sent to AI. This setting is now under Advanced Settings.

· Default 15 means only 'Normal' events from the last 15 messages are sent
· 'Important' and 'Critical' events are always sent, regardless of depth
· Set to 0 to only send 'Important' and 'Critical' events

Usually no adjustment needed. Higher values = more tokens consumed.
tutorial.step6Content:
Controls where Horae injects state info in the conversation.

· System @D: Uses Tavern's system injection depth, similar to World Info
· Preset @D: Positions from the end of the whole preset; 0 means the very end, 1 means before the last entry
· Story Timeline Injection Mode: Defaults to the original merged injection; separate early injection is also available

If your preset includes summaries or World Info with similar functions, adjust this section or disable overlapping preset features.
tutorial.step7Content:
Customize various prompts to adjust AI behavior:
· System Injection Prompt — Rules for AI to output <horae> tags
· AI Smart Summary Prompt — Rules for batch timeline extraction
· AI Analysis Prompt — Rules for single-message deep analysis
· Plot Compression Prompt — Rules for summary compression

⭐ Two built-in presets, one-click switch:
· Default (Simplified) — For low-floor RP without auto-summary or vector memory; event ~80-130 chars, lower AI load and Token cost
· Summary/Vector (Detailed) — For high-floor RP with auto-summary or vector memory; event 150-280 chars with full 5W1H for plot compression and vector recall

Recommended to modify only after getting familiar with the plugin. Leave empty for defaults.
tutorial.step8Content:
Custom Tables are an optional extension for Excel-style tables that AI fills on demand (e.g. skill tables, faction tables).

They are not required setup: timeline, characters and items are built-in tracking features, so you do not need to create tables before using Horae.

Key tips:
· Headers must be clearly written — AI uses them to understand what to fill
· Each table's 'fill prompt' must be specific for accurate results
· Some models (e.g. free-tier Gemini) have weaker table comprehension
tutorial.step9Content:
The following features are off by default, suited for detailed RP:

· Scene Memory — Records fixed physical features of locations for consistent descriptions
· Relationship Network — Tracks relationship changes between characters (friends, lovers, rivals, etc.)
· Mood Tracking — Tracks emotional/psychological state changes
· RPG Mode — Enables stat bars (HP/MP/SP), attribute radar charts, skill lists and status tracking

Enable these in 'Content Sent to AI' settings as needed.

If you previously edited Custom Prompts, reset or clear the corresponding custom prompt after enabling a new module, so the default prompt can include the new module rules.
tutorial.step10Content:
For new conversations, no extra setup needed — the plugin auto-attaches tags to AI replies and builds the timeline.

To review this tutorial, find 'Restart Tutorial' at the bottom of Settings.

Happy RP! 🎉

vector.title: Vector Memory (Semantic Search)
vector.hint:
Use vector search on Horae tag data to auto-recall relevant history for AI.
Local Mode: Downloads ~25MB quantized model to browser cache, uses 50-80MB memory. Not recommended for devices with <2GB RAM or cloud taverns. Ensure --max-old-space-size is configured.
API Mode: Uses remote Embedding API, no local resources needed. Supports OpenAI Embeddings and Gemini v1beta/batchEmbedContents; for official Gemini, enter the Google Generative Language API base URL.
vector.quantizationHint: q4 suits most devices; q8 has higher accuracy but double the size.
vector.apiWarning:
Only Embedding models allowed!
Do NOT enter LLM chat models (like gemini 3, gpt-4, etc.) or errors will occur.
Recommended: qwen3-embedding-8B
vector.fetchModelsHint:
Fill in URL and key, then click to fetch.
Recommended: qwen3-embedding-8B
vector.pureModeHint:
For high-dimensional API models (e.g. Qwen3-Embedding, text-embedding-3-large).
Disables keyword expansion, event phrase matching, high-frequency penalty, etc.
Relies fully on model's semantic understanding, reducing low-quality noise.
Do NOT enable for small or low-dimensional models, as recall quality will degrade.
vector.debugLogHint: When enabled, prints detailed recall info (thresholds, frequency filtering, dedup, Rerank steps) to the browser console. Use only while diagnosing issues; keep it OFF for normal use.
vector.rerankHint:
After vector recall, use a Rerank model for fine-grained re-ranking, significantly improving recall accuracy.
Pushes truly relevant results to the top.
Requires an additional Rerank model API (different from Embedding).
Recommended: Qwen3-Reranker, bge-reranker-v2-m3, bce-reranker
vector.rerankFetchHint:
Fill in URL and key, then click to fetch.
Recommended:  qwen3-Reranker-4B / 8B
vector.rerankFulltextHint:
By default Rerank uses summaries. When enabled, uses original message fulltext for precision ranking.
Can capture details missed by summaries, but requires long-context model support.
Only for long-context Rerank models (e.g. Qwen3-Reranker, 32K).
bge-reranker-v2-m3 (≤1024) and bce-reranker (≤512): Do NOT enable.
vector.rerankCandidatesHint: When Rerank is enabled, recall this many candidates with a loose threshold as the pre-rerank candidate pool. After Rerank sorts them, the Top-K setting above still controls how many final results enter the context. Recommended 20~40.
vector.rerankRecallThresholdHint: The embedding recall threshold used when Rerank is enabled, much lower than the "Minimum similarity threshold" above. Lets messages with low embedding score but high actual relevance reach the reranker.
vector.rerankMinScoreHint: Rerank results below this score are dropped and won't enter the context. Score scales vary across models: strict ≈ 0.85, balanced ≈ 0.5, lenient ≈ 0.2 — adjust to match the model you use.
vector.debugHint: Shows the query, hits at each stage, rerank inputs/outputs and final injected text from the most recent vector recall — useful for tuning and debugging.
vector.debugEmpty: No recall recorded yet. Send a message and come back.
vector.newRecallPresetPrompt: Enter a new recall parameter preset name:
vector.recallPresetHint: Selecting a preset immediately applies the current recall parameters. Small model preset is for local models and free Embeddings; large model preset is for high-dimensional API Embeddings; Rerank preset uses a larger candidate pool before precision ranking. Built-in presets cannot be overwritten or deleted.
vector.topKHint: Controls how many memories are finally injected into context per message. Without Rerank, this directly takes the top K vector recall results; with Rerank, it takes the top K results after reranking.
vector.thresholdHint: Memories below this threshold won't be recalled. Increase to reduce noise.
vector.fulltextCountHint: Top N results meeting the fulltext threshold send original text instead of summaries. Set to 0 to disable.
vector.fulltextThresholdHint: Only high-confidence results meeting this threshold send fulltext, avoiding token waste on irrelevant content.
vector.stripTagsHint:
Comma-separated tag names. Content wrapped in these tags will be removed in:
· Vector search fulltext review & Rerank fulltext precision
· Timeline parsing: prevents sideplay horae tags from polluting main timeline
· AI smart summary: strips before sending to AI
HTML comments are stripped by default.

vectorRecall.header: [Memory Recall — historical fragments related to the current scene, for reference only, not part of the current context]

prompts.title: Custom Prompts
prompts.hint: Customize rule prompts sent to AI. After modification, AI will output tags according to your rules. Use preset archives to quickly switch between character card configs.

builtinPresets.defaultDesc: Use for low-floor RP without auto-summary or vector memory; event ~80-130 chars, lower AI writing load
builtinPresets.vectorSummaryDesc: Use for high-floor RP with auto-summary or vector memory; event 150-280 chars with full 5W1H for plot compression and vector recall quality

ui.insertSummaryPlaceholder:
Enter summary content to replace deleted timeline events...

Note: Do not delete the initial timeline events, or relative time calculations and age progression will break.
ui.eventCompressDesc: Compress from extracted event summaries. Fast, but info limited to what's recorded in the timeline.
ui.fulltextSummaryDesc: Re-reads full message text for richer details, but consumes more tokens.
ui.clickToToggleScope: Click to toggle scope (local → character → global)
ui.tablePromptPlaceholder: Prompt: Tell AI how to fill this table...
ui.multipleEventsNote: This message has {{n}} events, showing first only
ui.generatingFulltextSummary: Re-reading full text to generate summary...
ui.scopeGlobalDesc: Global table, shared across all chats
ui.scopeCharacterDesc: Character table, bound to current character card
ui.scopeGlobalFull: Global (shared across all chats, data stored per character card)
ui.scopeCharacterFull: Character (structure bound to card, data per-chat)
ui.skillsInCard: Skills are shown in the character card above. Click + to add manually.
ui.noSkillsAddManually: No skills. Click + to add manually.
ui.noReputationCategories: No reputation categories. Click + to add.
ui.noReputationValues: No reputation data (auto-updates after AI reply)
ui.noEquipCharData: No character data (auto-updates after AI reply, or add manually)
ui.noCustomTemplates: No custom templates (built-in templates cannot be deleted)
ui.noStrongholds: No strongholds (click + to add, or AI creates via base: tags in <horae>)
ui.noLevelData: No level data (auto-updates after AI reply, or click the button above to add manually)
ui.sameSourceTarget: Source and target cannot be the same
ui.npcNameChangePlaceholder: After renaming, old name is auto-saved as alias
ui.pinAsMainChar: Mark as main character (pinned + special border)
ui.aiEnrichHint: ✨ button: Search history for this name and let AI extract appearance/personality/relationship. For reference only — review and edit before saving.
ui.quickScanTitle: Extract formatted data from text (no API cost)
ui.aiAnalyzeTitle: Use AI to analyze message content (costs API)
ui.templateReplaceHint: Loading will replace this character's slot config. You can still add/remove individual slots after.
ui.templateManageHint: Built-in templates are not listed here. Below are user-saved templates.
ui.reputationSubitemHint: Sub-item names are sent to AI to guide reputation breakdowns; existing sub-item values are injected into the current state. Leave empty to track only the total value.
ui.rpgKeyHint: Internal key: lowercase letters, numbers, and underscores only. Put localized meaning in the display name/description.
ui.rpgDisplayNameHint: Display name / localized meaning: shown in the UI and sent to AI.
ui.rpgDefinitionHint: Definition: sent to AI to explain what this attribute means.
ui.rpgBarRequiredHint: When checked, AI is asked to write this status bar every turn.
ui.equipmentFormHint: Current form; switching forms does not unequip items. Incompatible equipment is marked inactive.
ui.equipmentInactiveReason: Current form '{{form}}' does not include this slot; the equipment is kept but not injected to AI.
ui.localVectorWarningP1: Embedding model is very large (~400MB), loading in browser will use a lot of memory and may crash.
ui.localVectorWarningP2: Strongly recommend switching to "API Mode" (e.g. SiliconFlow free embedding model) for zero memory pressure.
ui.pendingScanDesc:
Last AI scan wrote data for {{n}} messages but review was not completed.

Choose an action:
ui.tokenLimitHint: Larger value means more messages per batch and fewer generations, but may exceed model limits.
ui.extractInfoHint: Extract info from history text. After extraction, review and adjust each item in the review dialog.
ui.stripTagsHint: Comma-separated tag names. Matched blocks will be removed before sending to AI (e.g. side scenes).
ui.stripTagsHint2: Also applies to timeline parsing and vector retrieval, synced with vector settings.
ui.tokenModeHint: Enter the token trigger threshold. Auto-compression triggers when exceeded.
ui.tokenModeHint3: Recommend setting to 30%~50% of model context window, leaving room for other content.
ui.messageModeHint2: Only AI messages are counted; interleaved user messages are included in the full text but not counted. Counting stops at summary boundaries.
